criteria.label: Define bias in the bias–variance decomposition and distinguish it from fairness bias.
criteria.example: Write: bias is (E[ŷ] − f*(x)) — systematic error from model assumptions; not demographic unfairness.

criteria.label: State the three terms in expected squared prediction error.
criteria.example: List bias², variance of ŷ, and irreducible error Var(ε) for E[(y − ŷ)²].

criteria.label: Diagnose high bias from train and validation error patterns.
criteria.example: Given train MSE 1.2 and val MSE 1.3 with a linear model on curved data, explain underfitting and propose polynomial features.

criteria.label: Explain why more data alone does not fix high bias.
criteria.example: Sketch a learning curve: val error plateaus high while train error also stays high as n grows — structural misspecification.

criteria.label: Recommend a bias-reducing change for an underfitting model on tabular data.
criteria.example: Switch from depth-1 stump to gradient-boosted trees with depth 4, or add interaction features, then compare train/val MSE.
